title: Pelvic Pain and Fever - PID
chief_complaint: 25-year-old sexually active female with lower abdominal pain, purulent vaginal discharge, fever 38.5°C, and dyspareunia for 5 days. Multiple sexual partners, inconsistent condom use.

physical_exam.pelvic: Purulent cervical discharge, cervical motion tenderness, bilateral adnexal tenderness, no masses

hidden_data.cervical_swab: Chlamydia trachomatis PCR: Positive, Gonorrhea: Negative
hidden_data.us_pelvis: No tubo-ovarian abscess, fluid in cul-de-sac

key_learning_points: CDC criteria: uterine/cervical motion tenderness + adnexal tenderness
Abstain from intercourse until treatment complete

patient_responses.pain: Lower abdominal pain, worse with sex
patient_responses.partners: I have 2 partners, don't always use condoms

feedback.incorrect_message: This is PID. The cervical motion tenderness + adnexal tenderness confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: PID: Ceftriaxone 500mg IM once + Doxycycline 100mg BID x14 days + Metronidazole 500mg BID x14 days. Partner treatment. Abstain until treatment complete. Follow-up in 72h. Reportable disease.
